title: Chapter Thirteen: Insight
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"My thing, you say?"

"Aye. Each squad of the Divine Sword Unit receives supplies -- spirit medicines, spirit pills. Not grand items. But this one is yours."

Ma Jin tossed the spirit pill. Yeon-shin caught it. Stared at the white lump in his palm.

He had never seen anything like it at the Jeong estate. A small martial arts clan in the countryside did not have spirit pills. It was not that his father had hated him. There had simply been nothing to give.

"With your level of internal energy, you should see significant effects," Ma Jin said. "Have you ever consumed spirit medicines before?"

"No."

"Dissolving the medicinal energy is not possible with ordinary qi circulation. I will guide you. Let us go."

"Chief."

"Hm?"

"Why are you being so kind to me?"

The question hung in the air. The kind of question only a boy with nowhere else to go could ask.

The scar across Ma Jin's face twitched.

"I am not giving you special treatment. It is the same for everyone in Radiant Demon Wing. You will understand after you risk your life once or twice."

He tapped Yeon-shin's shoulder. Walked past.

Yeon-shin stared at his back for a moment. Then followed, nudging Heon Won-chang's arm as he passed.

---

Inside the Radiant Demon Wing Hall, the blue-rank masters had taken up positions around the building. Cheong Myeong on the roof. Baek Mi-ryeo conversing casually with a visitor, never leaving her post. A security net, subtle and lethal.

In the centre of it, Yeon-shin sat cross-legged. Ma Jin's hand rested on his back.

"Imagine connecting the Ren and Du meridians end-to-end in the upper body. Place the medicinal energy in the flow of true energy and circulate until it dissolves. I will intervene if your circulation becomes unstable."

"Aye."

He put the pill in his mouth. Chewed. No taste.

The energy began to emit.

Two cups of tea later, the circulation was complete. Ma Jin had not needed to assist. His face had settled into an expression of resigned acceptance.

"Congratulations. You absorbed it completely. All of the medicinal energy."

"The increased true energy is remarkably robust," Yeon-shin said, observing his internal state. "Incomparable to what I had before."

"I am amazed you fought with such little energy. Now you have true qi befitting the white rank." Ma Jin shook his head slowly. "Your original internal energy was pitiful. Worse than the Desolate Sect standard-issue spirit pills could account for. How you managed to fight at all is beyond my understanding."

"The Fate Conquering Scripture compensates," Yeon-shin said. "It doubles the efficiency of whatever energy I possess. With the spirit pill's addition, I feel as if I have been given a new body."

He clenched and opened his fist. The true energy flowed through his meridians like clean water through newly carved channels -- stronger, denser, more responsive. Every technique he possessed would benefit.

*And one must live in order to repay favours, both large and small.*

He looked up at Ma Jin.

"I want to go out into the martial world," Yeon-shin said.

---

He bowed to the seniors over a dozen times. Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo touched his shoulders and ruffled his hair with the casual physicality of people who had long since stopped caring about martial protocol.

*Excessive physical contact between martial artists is usually uncomfortable. Who knows what they might do to one's insides.*

But the warmth was genuine, and the pressure behind his crown gate pulsed -- not with pain, but with something else. Something unfamiliar.

He returned to the dormitory. Heon Won-chang gave him a look.

"You are going to train again?"

"Aye."

*The main training hall is too burdensome right now.*

He walked to the small training hall -- the labyrinthine building without a ceiling where Radiant Demon Wing's top experts practised secret techniques. Energy waves pulsed from within. He passed occupied rooms, searching for an empty one.

A transmitted voice stopped him.

*Youngest!*

Not spoken aloud. Etiquette in the small training hall -- no one disturbed a possible epiphany.

He turned. Baek Mi-ryeo stood behind him, true energy rolling from both hands, her sheathed sword at her waist.

*What is it, Senior?*

His voice transmission was clumsy -- a gentle breeze where it should have been a solid bolt. Lacking internal energy, lacking fire control.

*I need a partner. Care to receive a move?*

*Did you have an epiphany? Congratulations.*

*A small spark. You would not say you cannot spar with me after that duel, would you?*

*Will it not be too loud?*

*Let us go.*

They left the small training hall and walked in silence to the main ground.

"I apologise if you had planned training," Baek Mi-ryeo said when they were in the open. "An idea came to me, and I am in a hurry."

"On the contrary, this is better. I can train alone anytime, but receiving pointers from Senior Baek is not a common opportunity."

"My specialty is swordsmanship, so why are you talking about fist techniques?"

"You were focusing on both hands while carrying a sheathed sword," Yeon-shin said. "If you were practising the draw, that would be different. With your skill, you could unleash a swift sword from any position."

She smiled. Cold eyes, but the smile fit her face.

"Just call me Senior Baek."

They faced each other in the training hall. A few others practised around them, unconcerned.

"I will take the first move," Yeon-shin said.

He drew.

Her hand closed on his wrist before the blade cleared the scabbard. A blur of motion -- Grappling Seize, impossibly fast. This was the strength of a blue-rank warrior who was not caught off guard.

His left hand remained. He struck along the trajectory drawn by his true energy, but the distance was too close for power. Their bodies pressed together.

Her backhand struck his abdomen.

The blow was a thunderclap. At that distance, with that force -- it should have been impossible. He was shoved backward, his wrist still locked in her grip.

Radiant Demon Formation moved his body before his mind could follow. His knee rose, true energy surging through it, and struck her arm free.

Baek Mi-ryeo rubbed her forearm. Surprised. Satisfied.

"You used the true energy in your footwork elastically," she said. "The efficiency of the inch strike has improved. I can put more force into it."

"Have you been practising inch strike?"

"A swordsman who has mastered Radiant Demon Formation cannot rely solely on the sword. Inch strike is useful in close quarters. If the opponent has layered energy on their body, you break the protective layer first."

Yeon-shin hesitated. Then spoke carefully.

"May this newcomer say a word? I have had sensitive energy perception since I was young, and the way you utilise true energy caught my eye."

"You can see the movement of true energy?"

"I observe the flow in footwork and the waves spreading outward. I cannot see true energy inside the body."

"Even that is remarkable. Such perception belongs to the purple rank."

She studied him. Her gaze weighed him and found him worth the gamble.

"Speak, youngest."

"Your stance seems more suitable for internal strength than inch strike. The emission and reception of true energy itself matches the patterns of heavy-palm technique. If you had used internal strength with the same energy --"

He stopped. Her eyes had widened.

"Are you already at that level of mastery in heavy-palm techniques as well?"

Silence.

---
